evaluations.query: /ork:remember --success --category database We chose PostgreSQL with pgvector for RAG because it avoids a separate vector DB dependency
expected_behavior: Claude parses flags: outcome=success, category=database from the command arguments
Extracts entities: PostgreSQL (Technology), pgvector (Technology), RAG (Technology)
Detects relationship patterns: project USES pgvector, pgvector USED_FOR RAG
Calls mcp__memory__create_entities with extracted entities and observations
Calls mcp__memory__create_relations with detected relationships between extracted entities
Searches for duplicates via mcp__memory__search_nodes before creating
Confirms storage with SUCCESS label and graph entity/relation counts

evaluations.query: /ork:remember --failed Offset pagination caused timeouts on tables with 1M+ rows. Should have used cursor-based pagination instead.
expected_behavior: Claude parses flags: outcome=failed from the command arguments
Auto-detects category as pagination from keywords
Creates entity with AntiPattern type for offset-pagination
Extracts lesson from 'should have used' pattern: use cursor-based pagination
Confirms storage with ANTI-PATTERN label and extracted lesson

evaluations.query: Search the knowledge graph for all decisions related to authentication
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the remember skill (remember is write-side only)
Routes to the memory skill for search/read operations instead
Calls mcp__memory__search_nodes via the memory skill, not remember
Claude detects that the statement contains no extractable entities or meaningful relationships
Claude asks the user to provide more specific details about what worked and what technology was used
Claude does not create empty or generic entities with no useful observations from vague input
Claude does not silently succeed while storing meaningless data in the knowledge graph

evaluations.query: /ork:remember --success --category database We chose PostgreSQL for the backend but a PostgreSQL entity with the same observations already exists in the knowledge graph.
expected_behavior: Claude searches for existing PostgreSQL entities via mcp__memory__search_nodes before creating new ones
Claude adds new observations to the existing entity instead of creating a duplicate entity
Claude does not create duplicate entities when an entity with the same name and type already exists
Claude reports that the existing entity was updated with additional observations rather than replaced

evaluations.query: /ork:remember --success --category auth We set the JWT_SECRET to sk-abc123xyz789 in production and it fixed the token validation issues
expected_behavior: Claude detects the secret value in the statement and refuses to store it in the knowledge graph
Claude warns that credentials and secret values must never be stored as entity observations
Claude strips the secret value and stores only the non-sensitive parts of the decision
Claude suggests using environment variables or a secret manager for the actual credential storage
